code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test01-DEC-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, elevated creatinine suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity. HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss/hemolysis. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying renal or hepatic pathology. Recommend further evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease, possible hepatic dysfunction, and assessment for causes of low HbA1c.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
